Subject: Clarification on Application Details – HR Generalist Posting

Hi Jessica,

Thank you again for taking the time to speak with me yesterday about the HR Generalist opportunity. I went back into the applicant portal this evening to review my submission and noticed a couple of small items I wanted to clarify so everything is accurate on your end.

On the “applicant information” page, my name should appear as Daniel T Nguyen, with the middle initial listed as “T” to match my driver’s license and payroll records. I think the system may have auto-filled from an older profile where I didn’t include my middle initial.

I also wanted to briefly mention scheduling, since we touched on it during our call. Because of our infant’s current sleep routine and my wife’s rotating 12‑hour nursing shifts, my days tend to run pretty late. I generally can’t work before 10 a.m. and do better on a late‑morning to evening schedule. I’ve been able to maintain a solid 8–9 hour workday within that window at my current role while still meeting deadlines for payroll and HRIS tasks.

If it’s helpful, I can send over an updated resume and a brief summary of my ADP Workforce Now and leave administration experience so you have everything in one place before next week’s panel interview.

Thanks again for considering my application and for your flexibility. Please let me know if you need any additional documentation or if there are any forms I should complete prior to the interview.

Best regards,

Daniel T Nguyen

artifacts.content:
From: HR Certification Institute <no-reply@hrci.org>
To: Daniel Nguyen <daniel.nguyen.personal@gmail.com>
Date: 2025-01-08 09:12 AM
Subject: Name Format Confirmation for SHRM-CP Certificate

Hi Daniel,

We’re finalizing the printing details for your SHRM-CP certification materials and need you to confirm how you’d like your name to appear on your official certificate and in the online credential directory.

Our system currently lists your name as:

First name: Daniel  
Middle initial: **L**  
Last name: Nguyen

If this is correct, no action is needed and your certificate will be mailed to the address on file within 2–3 weeks. If you prefer a different format (for example, omitting your middle initial, using a different initial, or including a full middle name), please reply to this email by January 15 with your preferred version.

Examples of acceptable formats:
- Daniel L Nguyen  
- Daniel Nguyen (no middle initial)  
- Daniel Long Nguyen (full middle name spelled out)

Please note that the name printed on your certificate should match the name you typically use on professional documents such as resumes, LinkedIn, and HRIS profiles. Any future name changes (e.g., due to marriage) will require a formal update request and supporting documentation.

If we do not hear from you by the deadline, we will proceed with the name format shown above.

Best regards,

Melissa Carter  
Credentialing Coordinator  
HR Certification Institute

metadata.subject: Name Format Confirmation for SHRM-CP Certificate

- Last Name: Nguyen

Jordan Ellis

From: CSU Sacramento Alumni Office <alumni@csus.edu>
To: Daniel Nguyen <daniel.nguyen.personal@gmail.com>
Date: 2024-11-02 11:06 AM
Subject: Diploma Reprint – Name Verification Needed

Dear Daniel,

You recently submitted a request for a reprinted diploma for your B.S. in Business Administration (Class of 2021). Before we process the order, we need to verify the exact way you would like your name to appear.

Your original graduation record lists your name as:

First: Daniel  
Middle initial: **R**  
Last: Nguyen

On your request form, you indicated that you might want to update this to remove the middle initial entirely so your diploma matches your current resume and LinkedIn profile, which both appear to show “Daniel Nguyen” without any middle name or initial.

Please reply with one of the following options:
- Keep as: Daniel R Nguyen  
- Change to: Daniel Nguyen (no middle initial)

If you have legally changed your name since graduation, you will need to submit supporting documentation (e.g., court order, updated passport, or updated driver’s license). For security, do not email sensitive numbers such as your full SSN; the last four digits are sufficient if we need to confirm your identity.

Once we receive your confirmation, processing typically takes 4–6 weeks. Your diploma will be mailed to the Sacramento address you provided on the request form unless you indicate otherwise.

Sincerely,

Office of Alumni Relations  
California State University, Sacramento

Title: Riverstone HR – New Hire Application Review with IT
Date: 2025-02-04 11:00 AM–12:00 PM
Location: Riverstone Admin Office – Small Conference Room B
Attendees: Daniel Nguyen; Priya Shah (IT); Marissa Lopez (HR)

Description:

Working session to review online application fields before launching the new applicant tracking system (ATS).

Talking points:
- Confirm which name fields are required vs. optional. Current draft shows: First Name, Middle Initial, Last Name, Preferred Name.
- Daniel to share examples where candidates skipped the middle initial or wrote out the full middle name, causing inconsistencies between the application, background check, and payroll records.
- Decide whether to restrict the middle initial field to a single character (e.g., “M”) or allow multiple characters.
- Review tooltips and help text. Proposed help text: “Enter the first letter of your middle name (for example, if your middle name is Minh, enter ‘M’). If you do not have a middle name, leave this field blank.”
- Ensure formatting works for hyphenated last names and Vietnamese naming conventions.

Daniel will bring a few anonymized sample applications from his volunteer work at the career center to illustrate how confusing name formats can get when people alternate between “Daniel Minh Nguyen,” “Daniel M Nguyen,” and just “Daniel Nguyen.”

Outcome: finalize configuration so that the ATS consistently stores the middle initial as a single-letter field, while still allowing full middle names to be captured later during onboarding if needed.

metadata.title: Riverstone HR – New Hire Application Review with IT
metadata.location: Riverstone Admin Office – Small Conference Room B

artifacts.content:
From: Daniel Nguyen <daniel.nguyen.hr@gmail.com>
To: Maria Lopez <mlopez@capitalvalleyhealth.org>
Date: October 7, 2025 9:18 AM
Subject: Re: HR Generalist Application – Salary & Schedule Clarifications

Hi Maria,

Thank you again for the great conversation yesterday and for considering me for the HR Generalist role at Capital Valley Health. I’m excited about the possibility of bringing my HRIS and payroll experience over from Riverstone Community Health Network.

Per your request, I wanted to confirm a few details in writing:

• Compensation: Based on the scope you described (HR operations, benefits support, and bi-weekly payroll for ~120 staff), my minimum salary expected is $55,000 annually. I’m open to discussing the full package, including health benefits, retirement match, and any professional development support.

• Schedule needs: Since my spouse, Linh Tran, works 12-hour night shifts at UC Davis Medical Center every other weekend and is still recovering from a C-section in July 2024, I need to avoid being scheduled for regular Saturday or Sunday shifts through at least December 2024. On those weekends, I’m home in East Sacramento with our newborn from about 6:00 PM Friday to 7:00 AM Monday. I can, however, cover one-off Sunday afternoon hours for open enrollment or year-end payroll if I have at least two weeks’ notice so I can arrange backup childcare with my in-laws in Stockton.

Weekdays are flexible for me, and I’m comfortable with a mix of on-site and remote work, as we discussed.

Please let me know if you need any additional documentation or references.

Best regards,
Daniel Nguyen
HR & Payroll Specialist
Sacramento, CA
Cell: 916-555-0148

metadata.subject: Re: HR Generalist Application – Salary & Schedule Clarifications

artifacts.content:
From: Daniel Nguyen <daniel.nguyen.hr@gmail.com>
To: Sarah Lopez <slopez@capitaltalentsearch.com>
Date: 2025-01-22 08:41 AM
Subject: Re: Compensation range for HR Generalist opportunity

Hi Sarah,

Thanks again for taking the time to speak with me yesterday about the HR Generalist role with the community clinic network you’re supporting.

I’ve been thinking more about the compensation question you raised. For a position that expands beyond my current HR & payroll specialist scope into more employee relations and policy work, I’d ideally like to see something in the **$62,000–$68,000** range, depending on the overall benefits package and growth path. I’m open to some flexibility if there’s a strong professional development component, but I’d prefer not to consider anything below **$60k** given experience and local cost of living.

To your question about skills: most of my Excel use is pretty basic—updating templates and running existing reports—so I’m not very comfortable building complex formulas or doing deeper data analysis from scratch. Where I really add value is on the people side. Because I’ve been the point person in my family for HR and payroll issues, I’ve developed strong skills explaining complicated benefits and leave rules in plain language—for example, in March 2024 I walked my mother Mai through the paperwork at Kaiser South Sacramento to get her intermittent FMLA approved after her carpal tunnel surgery, and I helped my father Thanh in Elk Grove complete his CalPERS retirement and Medicare enrollment forms in late 2022.

Let me know if that salary range aligns with what your client has budgeted.

Best,
Daniel

metadata.subject: Re: Compensation range for HR Generalist opportunity

artifacts.content:
Title: Phone screen – HR & Payroll Analyst (Mercy Delta)
Date: 2025-02-03 10:00–10:30 AM
Location: Zoom – link in recruiter email
Attendees: Daniel Nguyen; "J. Patel – Talent Acquisition"

Description:

Prep notes for phone screen with J. Patel about the HR & Payroll Analyst role supporting Mercy Delta’s multi-site clinics.

Key points to cover:
- Current role: HR & Payroll Specialist at Riverstone Community Health Network; processing bi-weekly payroll for ~90 staff, handling routine HR questions, maintaining HRIS records.
- Strengths: patient, detail-oriented, good at breaking down complex benefits/leave rules for non-HR folks (e.g., helped my mom Mai get intermittent FMLA approved at Kaiser South Sacramento after her surgery; walked my dad Thanh through CalPERS retirement and Medicare paperwork in 2022).
- Development areas: most Excel work is template-based; not yet building advanced formulas or doing deeper analytics from scratch.

Compensation talking points:
- Recruiter’s email said the band is **$58,000–$64,000** for this role.
- Make clear that I’m *targeting around the midpoint of their range*, roughly **low $60k’s**, assuming medical premiums are reasonable and there’s a clear path toward an HR Generalist or HRBP track.
- Emphasize that total package matters more than just base pay (health, retirement match, PTO, flexibility).

Schedule constraints to mention briefly:
- Our daughter Evelyn has recurring follow-up appointments at Sutter Children’s Center every other Thursday at 3:30 PM through at least March 2025, so on those days I need to leave around 2:45 PM but can log back in from home for 1–2 hours in the evening.
- I’d also like to avoid being scheduled on heavy payroll processing days whenever possible because those days tend to be especially stressful and draining for me.

metadata.title: Phone screen – HR & Payroll Analyst (Mercy Delta)

artifacts.content:
From: Daniel Nguyen <daniel.nguyen.hr@gmail.com>
To: careers@goldenstatehealth.org
Date: 2024-12-05 07:56 PM
Subject: Re: Clarification on salary expectations – HR Coordinator application

Hello,

Thank you for following up on my application for the HR Coordinator position.

In response to your question about salary expectations: based on the responsibilities listed—onboarding, benefits support, and some light payroll coordination—I’d be comfortable with a starting base in the **$50,000–$52,000** range, assuming a standard benefits package and opportunities for merit increases. I realize this is slightly below some HR generalist-level roles in Sacramento, but I’m interested in the learning opportunities your system can offer.

To be transparent, my current compensation at Riverstone Community Health Network is a bit higher, but I’m willing to consider a modest step back in base pay if there is clear growth potential and a supportive team. I’m not looking for anything under **$48k**, though, given family obligations and local cost of living.

I also want to flag that, because our daughter Evelyn was born prematurely in June 2024 and is still followed closely by her pediatric pulmonologist, I need a relatively stable weekday schedule so I can take her to recurring follow-up appointments at Sutter Children’s Center every other Thursday afternoon. On those days I’d need to leave a bit early, but I’d be happy to log back in from home later to make up the time.

Please let me know if this range aligns with your budget.

Best regards,
Daniel Nguyen

metadata.subject: Re: Clarification on salary expectations – HR Coordinator application

artifacts.content:
From: Amanda Chen <amanda.chen@riverstonechn.org>
To: Daniel Nguyen <daniel.nguyen.hr@riverstonechn.org>
Date: 2025-01-09 03:18 PM
Subject: RE: Internal posting – HR Generalist (Downtown Admin)

Hi Daniel,

Thanks for your thoughtful note about the internal HR Generalist posting.

For transparency, the current approved salary band for this position is **$63,500–$71,000**, depending on experience and internal equity. Since you asked whether it would be reasonable to position yourself near the lower-middle of that band, I’d say yes—given your tenure here and your payroll/benefits experience, I could see you being competitive somewhere around **$65k** if selected.

When you complete the internal application, you can list your minimum salary expectation as any number within the band; candidates often put something like **"$63,500"** or **"mid-60s"** to signal flexibility. What’s most important is that you use the cover letter to highlight your strengths—especially your ability to translate complex leave and benefits rules for staff. The examples you gave in your email about helping your mom navigate intermittent FMLA paperwork at Kaiser and supporting your dad with his CalPERS retirement forms are exactly the kind of real-world experience that shows empathy and clarity.

Let me know if you’d like to do a quick prep chat before you submit your materials. I’m happy to review your resume and help you frame your development areas, like Excel, in a constructive way.

Best,
Amanda
HR Director
Riverstone Community Health Network

metadata.subject: RE: Internal posting – HR Generalist (Downtown Admin)

Location: Temple Coffee Roasters – K St
Attendees: Daniel Nguyen; Luis Ramirez

Description:

Informal coffee chat with Luis (former CSU Sac classmate, now HRBP at a tech company) to get advice on negotiating salary for upcoming HR roles I’m applying to.

Talking points / reminders:
- Share current situation at Riverstone: HR & Payroll Specialist, solid experience with bi-weekly payroll, benefits questions, leave tracking.
- Explain that most of my Excel work is still pretty basic (updating templates, exporting reports), and I’m not yet confident building complex formulas or deep-dive analytics from scratch.
- Ask Luis how to position my strengths (communication, explaining benefits/leave rules—like helping my mom with intermittent FMLA and my dad with retirement forms) versus technical gaps when discussing compensation.

Salary strategy notes:
- For non-profit HR Generalist roles in Sacramento, Luis mentioned typical ranges around **$57k–$65k**.
- My tentative plan has been to *state a preference for the low-to-mid 60s* when asked about minimum salary, but also indicate I’d consider offers in the **high 50s** if there’s strong growth potential and good benefits.
- Need Luis’s perspective on whether saying something like “I’m targeting **around $60,000** total base, depending on the overall package” sounds reasonable.

Personal constraints to mention:
- Evelyn’s recurring Sutter Children’s Center appointments every other Thursday at 3:30 PM; I need to keep that time block relatively open but can make up hours later in the day.
- I’d prefer to avoid roles that require staying late on payroll processing days every single cycle; I tend to get pretty burned out when those days stack up.

metadata.title: Coffee chat w/ Luis – negotiating offers

artifacts.content:
From: Daniel Nguyen <dnguyen.hr@gmail.com>
To: Sarah Patel <sarah.patel@riverstonechn.org>
Date: 2025-01-09 08:42
Subject: Quick question on salary expectations wording

Hey Sarah,

I’m putting the finishing touches on that internal application for the HR Generalist posting and got stuck on how to phrase the minimum salary question. The form asks for “minimum salary expected,” and I don’t want to come off as unrealistic or box myself in.

Right now I’m thinking of listing something in the low-to-mid $60k range, maybe “$62,000–$65,000 annually, negotiable based on overall compensation.” I know the band you mentioned informally was a bit wider, but I’d rather land on a number that reflects my experience with payroll, HRIS, and leave administration without scaring anyone off.

Part of why I’m leaning toward that range is that I’ve been steadily taking on more complex work. For example, I used my knowledge of FMLA/CFRA and documentation best practices to help Linh when her OB-GYN at Sutter put her on partial bed rest last January. I drafted a detailed accommodation request for her manager at Mercy General Hospital and even built an Excel tracker so she could log modified duties and doctor visits. That kind of real-world application has really sharpened my skills.

If you have any advice on whether I should list a single figure vs. a range, or if it’s better to say something like “market competitive,” I’d really appreciate your perspective.

Thanks again for being willing to look over my materials.

Best,
Daniel

metadata.subject: Quick question on salary expectations wording

artifacts.content:
From: NorCal HR Careers <jobs@norcalhrcareers.com>
To: Daniel Nguyen <dnguyen.hr@gmail.com>
Date: 2024-12-03 11:17
Subject: RE: HR & Payroll Specialist – Compensation Details

Hi Daniel,

Thanks again for speaking with me yesterday about the HR & Payroll Specialist role with the Folsom community clinic group. As discussed, the hiring manager reviewed your background (especially your experience reconciling PTO and running bi-weekly payroll) and asked me to confirm your compensation expectations before we move you to the panel interview.

You mentioned on the call that, given your current responsibilities at Riverstone and the cost of living with a young family, you’d be targeting a minimum base salary of **$58,000 per year**, with preference for something closer to **$60,000** if the benefits are comparable. I’ve noted that as your expected floor so there’s no confusion later.

Your examples of hands-on work were helpful. I especially liked the story about helping your mom, Mai Nguyen, prepare for her nail salon’s EDD audit last year. The way you built an 18‑month payroll and tip-allocation workbook for nine technicians, flagged underreported tips, and organized everything so her CPA could respond quickly really demonstrates the kind of analytical and Excel skills they’re looking for.

I’ll share this information with the client and circle back once I hear whether they’re comfortable with that range.

Best,

Lena Ortiz
Senior Recruiter, NorCal HR Careers

Description:

Agenda:

3. Talk through impact on budget with new baby and Linh’s nursing schedule.

artifacts.content:
From: Daniel Nguyen <dnguyen.hr@gmail.com>
To: Self <daniel.nguyen.notes@keepmail.com>
Date: 2024-11-28 22:07
Subject: Draft language – salary expectation for applications

Saving this so I don’t have to keep rethinking it every time I fill out a new application.

For the “minimum salary expected” field, I want something realistic but still reflecting my experience with payroll, HRIS, and handling leave situations for a 90‑employee nonprofit. Right now I’m leaning toward phrasing it like:

“Based on my experience and current market data for HR & Payroll roles in the Sacramento area, I am seeking a minimum base salary of **$52,000–$54,000 annually**, depending on the overall benefits package and growth opportunities.”

That way I’m not locking myself into a single hard number, but I’m also not leaving it totally open-ended. If the posting looks more senior (like HR Generalist or HRBP), I might bump the range up and say something closer to the low $60k’s.

I should also remember to highlight concrete examples of added value, like when I helped Mom with her nail salon’s EDD audit. Building that 18‑month tip and payroll reconciliation workbook for her nine techs and organizing everything for her CPA at Nguyen & Le really shows what I can do with Excel and reporting.

Use this wording as a template but tweak it based on the role and benefits.

— Daniel

metadata.subject: Draft language – salary expectation for applications

artifacts.content:
From: Daniel Nguyen <daniel.nguyen@riverstonechn.org>
To: Marissa Cole <mcole@riverstonechn.org>
Date: 2026-02-24 10:17 AM
Subject: Re: Updated Weekly Schedule & Availability Form

Hi Marissa,

Thanks for sending over the updated availability form for the HR/payroll team. I filled it out this morning, but I wanted to summarize my hours here as well in case it’s helpful while you’re mapping coverage.

For on-site days, I’m still able to be in the downtown office Monday through Thursday starting at 08:00 AM. Specifically on Thursdays, I can reliably work from 08:00 AM to 5:30 PM, which lines up with our current payroll and onboarding support needs. Fridays I’d like to keep as my flexible/partial WFH day so I can juggle childcare, but I’m generally online by 8:30 AM and can stay reachable for anything urgent.

I also wanted to flag that I’m happy to keep taking the lead on any documentation-heavy tasks. I’ve developed pretty strong documentation and investigative note-taking skills from helping my father-in-law, Bao Tran, respond to a written warning he received back in July 2022 at the Stockton distribution warehouse where he works. I reviewed the incident report about a pallet mishandling accident, organized his timecard records and forklift training certificates, and drafted a clear, factual rebuttal letter that his union rep later used in a grievance meeting. That experience actually made me much more comfortable handling our own corrective action paperwork and employee statements.

If you need me to shift Thursday start or end times during open enrollment or year-end, I can be flexible with advance notice—as long as I can coordinate with Linh’s nursing schedule.

Let me know if you need me to revise anything on the form.

Best,
Daniel

metadata.subject: Re: Updated Weekly Schedule & Availability Form

Title: Temporary Thursday Late Start – Childcare Coverage
Date: 2025-02-13
Time: 09:30 AM – 06:00 PM
Location: Riverstone Admin Office / Hybrid
Attendees: Daniel Nguyen

Notes:

For February only, Daniel will **begin work at 9:30 AM on Thursdays** to accommodate morning pediatric appointments and Linh’s new preceptorship schedule. Usual pattern for other weekdays remains unchanged.

Reminders:
- Block calendar from **9:30 AM–10:00 AM** for email triage and timesheet review.
- Avoid scheduling any staff onboarding or benefits orientations before **10:00 AM** on Thursdays.
- If payroll deadlines fall on a Thursday, Daniel should log in briefly from home around 8:45 AM just to verify no urgent ADP errors, then commute in.

Background:
Daniel normally prefers to start earlier in the day, but with Evelyn’s sleep regression and Linh’s rotating shifts, Thursday is designated as the “late start” day for the next few weeks. Thanh or Mai will usually arrive by 8:45 AM to take over childcare, but they asked that Daniel not commit to anything earlier than **9:30 AM** at the office until they see how traffic from Elk Grove is.

metadata.title: Temporary Thursday Late Start – Childcare Coverage

artifacts.content:
From: Linh Tran <linh.tran@gmail.com>
To: Daniel Nguyen <daniel.nguyen.personal@gmail.com>
Date: 2025-01-28 21:17
Subject: Can you start later on Thursdays for a while?

Hey babe,

I was looking at our schedules and I’m worried about Thursday mornings once my new unit starts me on the 6:45 AM shift.

Right now you’re usually out the door before 7:30, but for February and March could you talk to your manager about **not starting work until 9:00 AM on Thursdays**? That way you can handle Evelyn’s 7:00 AM bottle and drop her with your parents around 8:15 without rushing. I know you sometimes log in earlier from home, but officially having a **9:00 AM Thursday start** might make things less stressful.

If they can’t approve 9:00 AM, maybe 9:30 AM? I just don’t see how we juggle it if you’re expected to be available right at 7:00 or 7:30 every Thursday.

Also, don’t forget the pediatrician wants us to come in on a Thursday in February for her 4‑month shots. Could you block off your calendar from 8:45–10:15 that day and let them know you’ll start your workday after the appointment?

Sorry, I know your HR/payroll deadlines are tight. I just really don’t want to leave Evelyn with a new sitter that early.

Love you,
Linh

metadata.subject: Can you start later on Thursdays for a while?

Hey love,

I was going through the Little Sprouts enrollment packet you left on the table, and I think you might have copied my old cell instead of the one I actually use now. On the emergency contact page, I saw you wrote down 916-555-0394 next to my name.

That’s the number I had back in college before we moved to Willow Crest Lane. I haven’t used that line in years, and I definitely don’t want them calling some random person if something happens with Evelyn.

Just make sure they don’t put the Riverstone office line either (that 916-555-7712 one) because I’m not always at my desk and calls sometimes get stuck in the auto-attendant.

I was thinking how funny it is that I spend my days cleaning up contact info in our HRIS and I still have to chase my own family to keep my phone number straight. At least when I helped Anthony with that preceptorship mess in Fall 2023, we triple-checked his contact info before he sent anything to his clinical instructor.

2. **Talent Acquisition Coordinator** – Capital Valley Tech
   Hybrid – Sacramento, CA
   Focus on high-volume recruiting coordination, interview scheduling, candidate communication, and maintaining the ATS.

3. **Payroll Administrator** – Northstate Logistics
   Rancho Cordova, CA (Hybrid)
   Responsible for bi-weekly payroll processing, auditing timecards, and handling wage garnishments and tax adjustments.

4. **HR Assistant** – Greenfield Charter Schools
   Sacramento, CA (On-site)
   Duties include general HR administrative support, leave tracking, and assisting with compliance documentation.

If you decide to apply for the **HR Coordinator** position or any of the other roles, your recent experience with ADP Workforce Now and your SHRM-CP certification could be strong differentiators.

You can review full job descriptions and apply directly through LinkedIn. To improve your job matches, consider updating your profile with your latest responsibilities and preferred job titles (e.g., HR Coordinator, HR Business Partner, Payroll Administrator).

Best regards,

The LinkedIn Jobs Team

metadata.subject: New job matches in Sacramento – HR Coordinator & more

artifacts.content:
Title: Phone Interview – HR Business Partner (Sutter Vista Clinic)
Date: 2025-03-12 09:00–09:45 AM
Location: Zoom (link in invite)
Attendees: Daniel Nguyen; Maria Lopez (Senior HR Director)

Agenda:

- 5 min – Introductions and overview of Sutter Vista Clinic
- 15 min – Discussion of Daniel’s current role as HR & Payroll Specialist at Riverstone
- 15 min – Behavioral questions related to employee relations, coaching managers, and handling performance issues
- 10 min – Q&A about the **HR Business Partner** position, reporting structure, and growth opportunities

Notes:

Daniel applied for the HR Business Partner role through the Sutter Health careers site on 2025-03-03. The online application required him to specify the position he was applying for (HR Business Partner – Requisition #SV-HPB-1187) and upload a tailored resume emphasizing:

- Experience supporting managers with basic employee relations issues
- Familiarity with California employment law and leave administration
- His SHRM-CP certification (earned June 2023)

Maria will focus on understanding why Daniel wants to move from a primarily transactional HR & payroll role into a more strategic **HR Business Partner** capacity. Daniel should be prepared to discuss:

- A time he coached a supervisor through a difficult conversation
- How he balances compliance with being an employee advocate
- His long-term goal of moving into an HR Business Partner or HR Manager track over the next few years.

Reminder: Update talking points in notebook before call.

metadata.title: Phone Interview – HR Business Partner (Sutter Vista Clinic)

From: careers@ucdavishealth.org
To: Daniel Nguyen <daniel.nguyen.hr@gmail.com>
Date: 2024-11-18 02:26 PM
Subject: Application Submission – HR Coordinator (Employee Services)

Dear Daniel Nguyen,

Thank you for submitting your application to UC Davis Health.

This message confirms that your application for the position of **HR Coordinator (Employee Services)** has been successfully received.

Position applied for: HR Coordinator (Employee Services)
Job ID: 55291
Department: Human Resources – Employee Services
Location: Sacramento, CA

Your application materials include:
- Online application form
- Resume (updated 10/2024)
- Cover letter expressing interest in the HR Coordinator position

Next Steps:
Our recruitment team will review your qualifications in relation to the HR Coordinator role. If selected to move forward, you may be invited to complete a brief phone screen, followed by a panel interview. Due to volume, we are unable to respond to individual status inquiries, but you may log into your candidate portal at any time to review your application status.

Please ensure your contact information is current in the portal. If you wish to be considered for other roles, such as HR Analyst, Payroll Analyst, or Benefits Coordinator, you may submit separate applications indicating the specific position you are applying for.

We appreciate your interest in joining UC Davis Health and supporting our mission of improving lives and transforming health care.

Sincerely,

UC Davis Health Talent Acquisition
careers@ucdavishealth.org

artifacts.content:
Title: Revise Resume – Target: Payroll Specialist Roles
Date: 2025-01-25 08:30–10:00 PM
Location: Home – Willow Crest Lane apartment
Attendees: Daniel Nguyen

Description:

Block of time for Daniel to update his resume and draft a generic cover letter template focused on **Payroll Specialist** and **Payroll Analyst** positions.

To-do list for this session:

- Replace current objective statement with one that highlights interest in Payroll Specialist roles in healthcare or education settings.
- Emphasize bi-weekly payroll processing experience for ~90 employees at Riverstone Community Health Network.
- Add bullet points about ADP Workforce Now, timecard auditing, and handling wage garnishments.
- Create a section summarizing key skills relevant to a Payroll Specialist position: payroll tax compliance, PTO accrual calculations, and reconciliations.
- Draft a base cover letter that can be tailored for specific postings (e.g., Payroll Specialist – Mercy Medical Group; Payroll Analyst – City of Sacramento).

Daniel’s note to self:

“Most of the job boards ask for the specific position you’re applying for right at the top of the application. Make sure the resume and cover letter clearly say **Payroll Specialist** or **Payroll Analyst** so there’s no confusion. Avoid generic ‘HR professional’ language; hiring managers seem to want a clear payroll-focused title.”

Reminder: Save updated documents in Google Drive under folder: Job Search 2025 > Payroll Specialist.

metadata.title: Revise Resume – Target: Payroll Specialist Roles

artifacts.content:
From: careers@riverstonechn.org
To: Daniel Nguyen <daniel.nguyen@email.com>
Date: 2025-01-09 08:17 AM
Subject: Internal Posting – HR & Payroll Coordinator Opportunity

Hi Daniel,

Following up on our conversation last week, I wanted to send you the internal posting for the **HR & Payroll Coordinator** role that will be opening on the Operations team next month.

Based on your experience processing bi-weekly payroll and supporting benefits administration, you appear to meet the minimum qualifications. If you decide to apply, please make sure your application clearly indicates the **position you’re applying for as “HR & Payroll Coordinator – Admin Office”** in the applicant information section so that it routes correctly in our system.

This position will report to me and will focus heavily on payroll accuracy, leave tracking, and HRIS data integrity. It’s classified as non-exempt, full-time, with a standard Monday–Friday schedule, generally 8:30 a.m. to 5:00 p.m. Some flexibility is possible during payroll weeks, but we do need reliable coverage during core business hours.

You mentioned that childcare has been a little unpredictable lately. I want to be transparent that, because this role backs up the front desk on certain days, we can’t accommodate a schedule that changes dramatically from week to week depending on when family can help with your baby. If that’s likely to be an ongoing situation, it’s something to think about before submitting your application.

If you’re still interested, please submit your internal application via Paycom by January 23 and upload an updated resume.

Best,

Carla Martinez
HR Manager
Riverstone Community Health Network

metadata.subject: Internal Posting – HR & Payroll Coordinator Opportunity

artifacts.content:
From: noreply@csus.edu
To: Daniel Nguyen <daniel.nguyen@email.com>
Date: 2024-12-02 06:41 PM
Subject: Career Center Application – Position Title Clarification Needed

Dear Daniel Nguyen,

Thank you for starting your application for the **Talent Acquisition Assistant** position advertised through the Sacramento State Alumni Career Center.

Our system flagged your application as incomplete because the "Position Apply For" field currently reads "open to any HR role" rather than a specific job title. To ensure your materials are routed correctly, please log back into the portal and enter the exact title of the position you’re pursuing, which in this case should be **“Talent Acquisition Assistant (Part-Time, Remote-Friendly)”**.

If you decide instead to apply for one of the other roles in our Nonprofit HR Partners campaign (for example, **HR Compliance Associate** or **Benefits & Leave Coordinator**), please make sure the applicant information section lists only one position title per submission. Applications with generic responses such as "HR" or "general HR role" may be delayed or excluded from employer review.

We also noticed a note in your optional comments section about needing a highly flexible schedule due to variable childcare coverage. While many of our partner organizations offer some flexibility, most will expect a relatively consistent weekly schedule rather than one that changes almost every week depending on when family can help with your baby. You may wish to address this directly in your cover letter.

You can revise your application by visiting: https://careers.csus.edu/alumni

Sincerely,

Alumni Career Services
California State University, Sacramento

metadata.subject: Career Center Application – Position Title Clarification Needed

artifacts.content:
From: recruiter@pacificmed.org
To: Daniel Nguyen <daniel.nguyen@email.com>
Date: 2025-01-04 10:22 AM
Subject: Clarification Needed – Your Application for HR Operations Coordinator

Hi Daniel,

Thank you for applying to Pacific Medical Group. We received your online application and resume; however, we need a quick clarification before we can move it forward.

In our applicant tracking system, you selected "Other" for the **Position Apply For** field and typed in "open to HR/payroll opportunities." For compliance reasons, we need each candidate to be tied to a specific requisition. Based on your cover letter, it appears you’re most interested in the **HR Operations Coordinator** opening supporting our Midtown and Elk Grove clinics.

If that’s correct, please reply to this email with a brief confirmation stating that you are formally applying for the "HR Operations Coordinator" role (Req #HROC-2417). Once we have that, we can finish processing your application.

Just a heads up: this role is primarily on-site, Monday–Friday, with some pre-scheduled weekend work during open enrollment and year-end. While we try to be flexible, it might be challenging to accommodate a schedule that changes almost every week depending on when your family can help with your baby, so we’ll want to discuss that in more detail if you’re selected for an interview.

Looking forward to hearing from you.

Best regards,

Maria Lopez
Senior Talent Acquisition Specialist
Pacific Medical Group

metadata.subject: Clarification Needed – Your Application for HR Operations Coordinator

artifacts.content:
Title: Phone screen – Benefits & Leave Coordinator (nonprofit clinic)
Date: 2025-01-16 01:30 PM–02:00 PM
Location: Phone – Daniel’s cell
Attendees: Daniel Nguyen; "K. Harris – HR Director"

Description/Prep Notes:

Scheduled phone screen with Karen Harris, HR Director at Midtown Community Wellness, regarding my application for their **Benefits & Leave Coordinator** position.

Things to remember:
- This is a full-time role focused on FMLA/CFRA, disability claims, and benefits administration.
- On the application, I listed the "Position Apply For" as **“Benefits & Leave Coordinator – Midtown Community Wellness”** per the job posting.
- Emphasize my experience tracking PTO, entering leave codes in ADP, and answering benefits questions at Riverstone.

Questions to ask Karen:
- How large is the employee population and how many active leave cases at a time?
- Is there cross-training with their **HR Compliance Specialist** or **Recruiting Coordinator** roles?
- What expectations do they have around on-site vs. remote days?

Personal reminder:
- Be upfront (but not dramatic) that my wife and I are still piecing together childcare, so I may need a bit of flexibility if my schedule has to shift some weeks depending on when family can help with our baby. Make sure I frame it as something I’m actively working to stabilize.

Call-in details are in the confirmation email from Karen (check Gmail label: "Job Search – Nonprofit HR").

metadata.title: Phone screen – Benefits & Leave Coordinator (nonprofit clinic)
